ux_issues.description: The shared trip public endpoint (GET /shared/{token}) returned an HTML page rather than JSON with trip data, making it impossible to verify shared trip content programmatically. The share/unshare flow itself worked correctly.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider returning JSON by default or supporting Accept header content negotiation for the shared trip endpoint.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: Companion mode response for Varanasi food was not noticeably more concise than planning mode responses. Both were similarly detailed and lengthy (~300+ words). Companion mode is expected to be more concise since the user is actively traveling.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Asked about Varanasi food in CHAT_MODE_COMPANION. Response was very detailed with 5 food items, cultural context, and price breakdowns — similar verbosity to planning mode.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: Itinerary did not include explicit per-item or per-day cost estimates. While the AI referenced costs in conversation, the create_itinerary_items tool calls did not include price information in item descriptions for most items, making it hard for the user to track daily budget compliance from the itinerary alone.
ai_behavior_issues.context: 62 itinerary items created across 21 days. Most items mention 'budget-friendly' or 'cheap' but lack specific INR/USD cost estimates in the item descriptions.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The AI demonstrated strong budget awareness throughout the session. It correctly flagged the Taj Mahal entry fee as exceeding the daily budget and suggested Mehtab Bagh as a free alternative. Food recommendations were accurate with realistic INR prices, all vegetarian, and well within the $3/meal limit. Transport recommendations correctly focused on Sleeper Class and General class trains, avoiding expensive AC options. The AI also correctly identified Varanasi as largely vegetarian by default. The 21-day itinerary was comprehensive with 67 items covering all 5 cities. Minor gaps: no expert persona handoff occurred (the food topic could have triggered one), and companion mode responses were not noticeably more concise than planning mode.
